cases.title: Testing-plan attempt/audit row: live roster reflects Present, Late, Pending, and rejected attempts during Open session
preconditions: Testing-plan §3 Attempt/audit coverage block applies (FR-22, FR-29, BR-22, BR-23, AC-18).
Open class session exists with enrolled students and assigned Lecturer authenticated.
Fixtures include students for successful Present check-in, Late check-in, pending enrollment, and at least one failed attempt with structured reason code.
steps: Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance as assigned Lecturer and capture baseline counts and rows.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins as enrolled student A within present window; poll or re-fetch attendance roster.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins as enrolled student B after present window but before close; re-fetch roster.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins as non-enrolled or duplicate student to produce a rejected attempt with reason code; re-fetch roster.
Assert data.counts.present, data.counts.late, data.counts.pending, and data.counts.rejectedAttempts align with row-level attendanceStatus and latestAttemptOutcome fields.
cases.expected: Scenario-matrix maps to testing-plan Attempt/audit coverage: during Open session the live roster exposes checked-in students as Present or Late, unresolved enrollments as Pending, and failed attempts with structured reason per AC-18; counts and rows stay consistent without full page reload semantics at API layer.

cases.title: M09 Realtime Delivery publishes roster updates from M04 check-in and M05 attendance ledger with DB coherence
preconditions: Integration harness uses real DB transaction boundaries for attendance_records, check_in_attempts, and enrollments tables.
Open session exists with known sessionId and enrolled student roster.
M04 submitCheckIn and M05 applyAttendanceOutcome emit events consumed by M09 realtime delivery path.
steps: Query baseline roster via M05/M09 read path for the Open session; record pending count.
Invoke M04 submitCheckIn for enrolled student with valid token yielding Success outcome.
Assert AttendanceRecord row persisted as Present or Late and CheckInAttempt row has outcome Success.
Invoke M09 roster projection read and assert student row attendanceStatus updated and counts.present or counts.late incremented.

Submit a second check-in attempt for same student producing DuplicateCheckIn; assert M09 projection increments rejectedAttempts and row latestAttemptOutcome reflects rejection without mutating success status.
cases.expected: M09 module integration with M04/M05 boundaries delivers coherent live roster snapshot: successful check-in mutates ledger and projection counts; rejected duplicate attempt surfaces in rejectedAttempts without corrupting the original Present/Late record.

cases.title: GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance returns 200 live roster envelope with counts and row shape
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated with Authorization bearer token.
Open session with mixed Present, Late, Pending, and rejected-attempt students exists.
steps: Send GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance with Authorization header.
Validate HTTP 200 and JSON envelope shape {data, meta, error} with error null.
Assert data contains classSessionId matching path param, state Open, counts object with present, late, pending, and rejectedAttempts integer keys.
Assert each row in data.rows includes studentUserId, studentCode, displayName, attendanceStatus, checkInMethod when applicable, checkInAt when checked in, and latestAttemptOutcome.
Assert meta includes requestId and timestamp fields per API design §2.3.
cases.expected: Endpoint returns HTTP 200 with stable envelope schema per API design §4.5; data.state is Open and counts/rows payload matches FR-19 live roster contract for lecturer dashboard consumption.

cases.title: POST /v1/enrollments/import row-level acceptance feeds Pending rows on live roster payload
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is authenticated.
classSectionId exists with a session in Open state and assigned Lecturer available for roster read.
Import payload includes valid student rows and at least one row with StudentNotFound or equivalent rejection.
steps: Send POST /v1/enrollments/import with mixed valid and invalid rows for the target classSectionId.
Assert HTTP 200 with data.acceptedRows count and data.rejectedRows array containing rowNumber, code, and message for invalid rows.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance as assigned Lecturer.
Assert HTTP 200 roster envelope and verify each accepted import student appears in data.rows with attendanceStatus Pending.
Assert rejected import rows do not appear in roster and counts.pending reflects only accepted enrollments without successful check-in.
cases.expected: Enrollment import returns HTTP 200 with row-level rejection assertions per FR-04; accepted students surface as Pending rows on FR-19 live roster payload; rejected import rows are excluded from roster counts.

cases.title: Lecturer authenticates via login then fetches live roster for assigned Open session
preconditions: Lecturer credentials exist for POST /v1/auth/login.
An Open session exists in a section assigned to that Lecturer with enrolled students.
steps: Send POST /v1/auth/login with Lecturer credentials and capture accessToken from HTTP 200 envelope.
Send GET /v1/me with bearer token and assert Lecturer identity and assigned section scope.
Send GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance with bearer token.
Assert HTTP 200 with data.state Open, counts object present, and data.rows array for enrolled students.
cases.expected: Login returns HTTP 200 with accessToken; GET /v1/me confirms Lecturer scope; subsequent GET attendance succeeds with FR-19 live roster payload for the assigned Open session only.

cases.title: Primary FLOW-06 lifecycle: open session enables live roster that updates as students check in
preconditions: Lecturer account and Scheduled session fixture with enrolled students are available.
Student accounts exist for at least two enrolled students with valid credentials.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open as assigned Lecturer and assert session state Open.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance and record baseline pending count equal to enrollment size.
Student A completes POST /v1/check-ins with valid QR within present window.
Re-fetch GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance without closing the session.
Assert student A row shows attendanceStatus Present with checkInMethod QR and counts.present incremented; pending decremented.
Student B checks in during late window; re-fetch roster and assert Late status reflected in row and counts.late.
cases.expected: WF-01 open followed by FLOW-06 monitor live roster: dashboard payload transitions students from Pending to Present or Late as check-ins succeed; summary counts stay synchronized with row state during Open session.

cases.title: Alternate flow: rejected check-in attempts appear on live roster with reason before retry succeeds
preconditions: Open session with enrolled student exists.
Student is authenticated.
Expired or invalid QR token and fresh valid token fixtures are available.
steps: Student submits POST /v1/check-ins with expired qrToken.
Lecturer calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance and locates student row.
Assert latestAttemptOutcome reflects ExpiredQr (or equivalent) and counts.rejectedAttempts incremented; attendanceStatus remains Pending.
Student rescans and submits POST /v1/check-ins with current valid token.
Re-fetch roster and assert student transitions to Present or Late with latestAttemptOutcome Success and rejectedAttempts count unchanged or adjusted per single-student semantics.
cases.expected: FLOW-06 alternate branch: failed attempt surfaces on roster with structured rejection reason per AC-UI-05; subsequent successful check-in updates row to Present/Late without full page reload at API refresh layer.

cases.title: Live roster payload reflects session state transition Open to Closed with finalized Absent rows
preconditions: Open session fixture with mix of Present/Late and Pending students exists in integration database.
Assigned Lecturer auth context is available.
steps: Record beforeState: GET roster for Open session with data.state Open and pending count > 0.
Invoke M03 closeSession(sessionId, actor) or POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close.
Record afterState: GET roster for same sessionId.
Assert data.state is Closed.
Assert previously Pending students now show attendanceStatus Absent per FR-09 finalization.
Assert Present and Late rows are preserved unchanged.
cases.expected: beforeState Open exposes live updating roster; after Open to Closed transition roster reflects Closed state and FR-09 absent finalization on pending students while preserving resolved Present/Late records.

cases.title: Student, ITAdmin, and out-of-scope Lecturer denied live roster endpoint
preconditions: Student, ITAdmin, and Lecturer-not-assigned-to-section test accounts exist with valid tokens.
An Open session exists in a section the out-of-scope Lecturer is not assigned to.
Assigned Lecturer token is available as positive control.
steps: Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance as Student.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance as ITAdmin.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance as out-of-scope Lecturer.
Read HTTP status and error.code for each denied request (expect 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope).
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance as assigned Lecturer and assert HTTP 200 with roster payload.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance as SystemAuditor and assert read-only scoped access per permission matrix §3.1.
cases.expected: Unauthorized roles cannot retrieve live session roster per View live session roster matrix; assigned Lecturer, AcademicAdmin, and scoped SystemAuditor may read roster; no cross-scope row leakage to denied actors.

cases.title: Live roster access for non-Open session returns appropriate state or denial without stale Open counts
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Session fixtures exist in Scheduled and Closed states with enrollment data.
steps: Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{scheduledSessionId}/attendance for a Scheduled session.
Record HTTP status and whether data.state is Scheduled with no live check-in counts implying active window.
Open and close a separate session; call GET /v1/class-sessions/{closedSessionId}/attendance.
Assert data.state is Closed with finalized roster rows; counts do not imply an active Open attendance window.
Verify non-existent sessionId returns 404 SessionNotFound without partial roster data.
cases.expected: FR-19 live dashboard semantics apply during Open session; Scheduled and Closed targets return roster payload with correct session state without exposing active live-window counts for non-Open sessions; missing session returns 404.

cases.title: Concurrent student check-ins during Open session produce consistent roster counts
preconditions: Open session exists with at least five enrolled students and valid QR token.
Integration harness can dispatch parallel POST /v1/check-ins from distinct enrolled students.
Assigned Lecturer auth context is established for roster reads.
steps: Record baseline GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance counts.
Fire parallel POST /v1/check-ins from five distinct enrolled students using the same valid token.
Await all responses and assert each distinct student receives Success outcome.
Immediately call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance.
Assert counts.present plus counts.late increased by five and counts.pending decreased by five with no duplicate success rows per student.
cases.expected: Concurrent successful check-ins during class-start burst converge on consistent roster counts and one success row per student per session; no lost updates or double-counting in live projection.

cases.title: PG-06 LiveRosterPanel renders summary chips and table columns with realtime row updates
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop Chromium.
An Open session is available on PG-06 with LiveRosterPanel (DC-06) mounted.
Enrolled students exist with at least one Pending row.
steps: Navigate to /lecturer/sessions/{sessionId}/roster (PG-06) and observe LiveRosterPanel.
Verify summary chips display present, late, pending, and rejectedAttempts counts sourced from attendance API.
Verify table columns include student identity (code + name), AttendanceStatusCell badge, and latest attempt outcome.
Trigger a student check-in from a separate client without reloading the lecturer browser tab.
Observe row and summary chip update within realtime delivery window without full-page reload.
cases.expected: Browser journey satisfies FLOW-06 and AC-UI-05: LiveRosterPanel shows count summary chips and roster table; student check-in event updates row status and chips without full page reload per NFR-UI-06.

cases.title: Rejected attempt row displays reason badge and tooltip on live roster
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop Chromium at PG-06 for an Open session.
A enrolled student has a recent failed check-in attempt with reason code such as NotEnrolled, DuplicateCheckIn, or ExpiredQr.
steps: Navigate to PG-06 live roster for the Open session.
Locate the student row with a rejected latestAttemptOutcome.
Verify AttendanceStatusCell shows Pending or unchanged success status while rejection is surfaced separately.
Hover or focus the rejection StatusBadge and confirm tooltip displays human-readable reason aligned with API error code.
Confirm rejectedAttempts summary chip count matches visible rejected rows.
cases.expected: PG-06 displays rejected attempts with reason badge and tooltip per DC-06 spec and AC-UI-05; lecturer can identify failure cause without leaving the live roster view.

cases.title: Closed session PG-06 shows finalized roster without live-update affordances
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop Chromium.
A Closed session fixture exists with mixed Present, Late, and Absent rows from prior open/close cycle.
steps: Navigate to /lecturer/sessions/{closedSessionId}/roster (PG-06).
Verify StatusBadge or session header shows Closed state.
Assert LiveRosterPanel displays finalized Absent rows for students without successful check-in.
Confirm no live-update spinner or websocket reconnect banner implies active Open-session polling.
Verify summary chips reflect finalized present, late, pending (zero), and absent counts.
cases.expected: PG-06 Closed state shows static finalized roster per FLOW-07 step 5; Absent rows visible after close processing; UI does not behave as an active live dashboard for non-Open sessions.
